mainEntity.name: How much does an OM615/OM617 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned OM615, OM616 or OM617 typically costs £2,000–£3,500 supply only, varying by displacement and specific variant. Fitting adds £800–£1,400 depending on labour rates. Full professional rebuilds can exceed £5,000.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes OM615/OM617 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM61x series is rated 4.0★ out of 5 — these engines are legendary for their bulletproof reliability and are capable of 500,000+ km (over 300,000 miles) with proper maintenance. The OM617, in particular, gained a reputation as one of the most durable diesel engines ever produced.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common OM615/OM617 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Common issues include rear main seal leaks (a known weak point that can lead to oil starvation), injection pump wear (Bosch M pump rack diaphragm failure on earlier units), and timing chain stretch requiring replacement every 200,000–250,000 km. Valves require adjustment every 15,000 miles due to the lack of hydraulic lifters.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes models use the OM615, OM616 and OM617?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM615 (1.9–2.2L four-cylinder) and OM616 (2.4L four-cylinder) powered the W114/W115 'Stroke 8' and W123 models as 200D, 220D and 240D. The OM617 (3.0L five-cylinder) powered the W123 300D, W116/W126 300SD, and W460 G-Class 300GD, both in naturally aspirated and turbocharged forms.

mainEntity.name: How long does the OM615/OM617 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With proper maintenance — regular oil changes, valve adjustments every 15,000 miles, and timing chain inspections — these engines are capable of 300,000–500,000 miles. Many examples remain on UK roads today, testament to their fundamental engineering quality.

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz OM615/OM616/OM617 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes OM615, OM616 and OM617 are legendary cast-iron diesel workhorses — among the most durable engines ever built, but they need regular valve adjustments and rear main seal attention. Backed by real UK enquiries from EnginesMarket.co.uk.

rows.value: W115, W123, W116, W126, W460 G-Class, T1/T2 vans

verdictRating.confidence: 90% confidence — based on verified specialist rebuild reports and UK workshop consensus.

rows.score: 16 (Well-understood, mechanically simple repairs; parts still available)
16 (Available from specialists; some parts becoming scarcer)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The OM61x series is one of the most durable diesel families ever built — but regular valve adjustments and rear main seal leaks are the main maintenance items — buy with confidence and a specialist inspection.
verdictRating.bestFor: Buyers wanting a classic Mercedes diesel with legendary longevity and mechanical simplicity.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You're not prepared for the slower pace and regular maintenance (valve adjustments every 15,000 miles) these engines require.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an OM615, OM616 or OM617 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). Specialist/classic engine rebuilds may cost £5,000–£10,000+ for a full professional overhaul.
